---
title: "Marketing Mix Model"
subtitle: "Phase 2 Update — April 28, 2026"
author: "Data Science Team"
format:
  revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    theme: default
    transition: none
    width: 1400
    height: 900
    margin: 0.06
    center: true
    hash: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    scrollable: false
    css: custom-reveal.css
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

# Marketing Mix Model

**Phase 2 Progress Review | April 28, 2026**

---

## Today

1. Data foundation confirmed
2. Channel landscape and incrementality benchmarks
3. Model structure built — Meta proof-of-concept
4. Three questions to finalize the full model next week

---

## Data Foundation

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Modeling window | **Q3 2024 – March 2026** |
| Total historical spend | **$112.9M** |
| Daily average spend | $132.5K |
| Mean daily conversions | **2,560** |
| Peak daily conversions | 4,354 (May 30, 2025) |
| Tax-season lift | ~2.4× rest-of-year |
| Spend–conversion correlation | r = 0.45 (same-day) |
| Corrected data files | Received 2026-04-27 ✓ |

*Modeling window rationale: pre-Q3 2024 spend has channel-level gaps being resolved by data engineering.*

---

## Channel Landscape

::: columns
::: {.column width="48%"}

![](../outputs/P1_02_eda/figures/top_10_channels_spend.png){width="100%"}

:::
::: {.column width="52%"}

| Channel | Share | Treatment |
|---|---|---|
| Meta | 37% | iOS / Android / Web |
| TikTok | 21% | iOS / Android / Web |
| Google Search | 16% | iOS / Android / Web |
| Linear TV | 7% | Separate |
| Applovin | 5% | Separate |
| CTV | 5% | Separate |
| Apple Search Ads | 4% | Separate |
| Podcast | 3% | Separate |
| DSPs (Liftoff, InMobi) | 2% | Grouped |
| **Idle / Others** | — | *Excluded — data artifact* |

:::
:::

---

## Incrementality Benchmarks

*These are our ground-truth anchors for how much each channel truly drives customer acquisition.*

| Test | Period | Platform | iCAC | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Meta — May 2025** | 9 days | iOS | **$135** | High — 12% holdout |
| **Meta — May 2025** | 9 days | Android | **$63** | High — 12% holdout |
| **Meta — May 2025** | 9 days | Web | **$157** | High — 12% holdout |
| TikTok — Aug/Sep 2025 | 6 weeks | iOS | $109 | Moderate |
| TikTok — Aug/Sep 2025 | 6 weeks | Android | $82 | Moderate |
| TikTok — Aug/Sep 2025 | 6 weeks | Web | $112 | Moderate |
| CTV — Oct/Nov 2025 | 4 weeks | All | $135 | Moderate |
| Meta — Jan 2026 | 5 days (cancelled) | Android | $155 | Low — included with wider CI |
| Meta — Jan 2026 | 5 days (cancelled) | Web | $240 | Low — included with wider CI |
| Meta — Jan 2026 | 5 days (cancelled) | iOS | — | *Excluded — result anomalous ($1,350 vs $135 in May)* |

**iCAC** = spend per customer acquired *incrementally* (customers we would not have gotten without that channel).

---

## Meta Model — What We Built

**Framework:** PyMC-Marketing (Bayesian MMM)

The model learns how spend in each channel translates to 1-year cohort LTV, accounting for:

- **Carryover effects** — ads influence behavior days after initial exposure
- **Diminishing returns** — doubling spend does not double customers
- **Seasonality** — the tax-season lift (January–April) is modeled explicitly

**What we've done:**

- Model specified for Meta iOS, Android, and Web (3 channels × 603 days)
- Model structure validated — prior predictive checks confirm the expected shapes
- Full model training runs this week once data questions are resolved

---

## ROAS Response Curves — Meta

![](../outputs/P2_01_pymc/figures/roas_response_curves.png){width="100%"}

*Dashed line = each channel's 52-week median weekly spend (current operating point). Shaded band = 80% uncertainty interval. Downward slope = diminishing returns.*

---

## What the Curves Tell Us

| Channel | 52-wk Median Spend | Shape Reading |
|---|---|---|
| Meta iOS | $58K/wk | Moderate returns at current spend; clear saturation zone visible |
| Meta Android | $117K/wk | In diminishing-returns zone; incremental return declining |
| Meta Web | $332K/wk | Highest spend; notable saturation; every additional dollar returns less |

**Why the uncertainty band is wide:** At this stage we're using the model's prior structure — the bands narrow significantly once we run full model training with the incrementality tests incorporated as data-informed priors. That's the version we deliver next week.

---

## Three Questions to Finalize the Full Model

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

**1. LTV file update**
New file shows uniformly higher LTV values. Same 1-year definition, or updated LTV model?
*Determines: how we label ROAS outputs*

**2. Decision timeline**
When do you next use budget allocation outputs for an actual spend decision?
*Determines: precision bar — directional vs. committed spend confidence*

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**3. Channel continuity**
Any new channels planned for Q2/Q3 2026? Is CTV still active after the Oct–Nov test?
*Determines: model scope for the planning horizon*

:::
:::

---

## Next Steps

::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}

**This week:**

- Re-run full model training on corrected data files
- Incorporate today's answers into model specification
- Begin TikTok and CTV channel models

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Next week:**

- All channels modeled with calibrated priors
- Channel-by-channel ROAS and iCAC curves
- Spend scenario analysis (e.g., shift 10% Meta → TikTok)

:::
:::

**Target:** Full channel model results by end of next week.
